PSG filename: subject identification
Method used to split the sleep period in sleep cycles, it defines the criteria. I.e. : "Minimum criteria"  "Aeschbach 1993"  "Feinberg 1979"
Include a REM sleep periods (REMP) that occur within 15 minutes of sleep onset.
Include the last sleep cycle even if the NREM period (NREMP) or REMP does not meet the minimum duration criteria.
Minimum length without R stage to end the REMP.
Minimum length of the first NREMP in minutes.
Minimum length of the middle and last NREMP in minutes.
Minimum length of the NREMP in minutes to validate the last sleep cycle.
Minimum length of the first REMP in minutes.
Minimum length of the middle REMP in minutes.
Minimum length of the last REMP in minutes.
Move the end of the REMP to the start of the following NREMP, eliminating the temporal "gap" between 2 cycles.
List of valid stages used to define the sleep cycles:  "N1, N2, N3, R" or "N2, N3, R"
Sleep stages selection to detect REMs in.
REMs event name (specific to the detection algorithm).
The label of the channel.
The sampling rate (Hz) of the channel.
Number of sleep cycles.
Recording duration (min) from lights off to lights on.
Total period for detection - Duration (min) of the sleep period.
Valid (no artifact) period for detection - Valid duration (min) of the sleep period in REM stage.
Total - REMs count in REM stage.
Total - Average REMs duration (s) in REM stage.
Total - Average REMs amplitude (uV) in REM stage.
Total - REMs density (count/epoch) in REM stage. Epoch duration is 30 seconds.
Total - Phasic REM percentage in REM stage. Ratio of total REM event duration to total REM stage epoch duration (%).
Total - Tonic REM percentage in REM stage. Complement of phasic REM percentage (100 - phasic REM percentage).
Total - Average peak-to-peak REM energy (uv squared) in REM stage. Per-event energy is amplitude_uV squared.
Total - Peak-to-peak REMs activity index in REM stage. Sum of per-event energies (amplitude_uV squared).
